sae_features.label: N-terminal phosphate-binding loops
sae_features.description: Conserved phosphate-binding loops in N‑terminal lobes of ATP-dependent enzymes of the actin/Hsp70/sugar‑kinase (ASKHA) superfamily and analogous phosphate‑coordinating loops in other phosphate‑metabolizing proteins (e.g., CD39/GDA1 ectonucleotidases, DHH/DHHA1 exopolyphosphatases); the feature keys on glycine/lysine/threonine‑rich segments that engage phosphoryl groups, including but not limited to Walker‑A motifs.

sae_features.label: C-terminal appendage detector
sae_features.description: C-terminal region/appendage detector: the feature marks the C-terminal half and tail of proteins—often flexible, polar/acidic and/or helical, and sometimes including a terminal transmembrane anchor—with strongest activation on actin/actin-like and tubulin/FtsZ cytoskeletal proteins; it generally avoids the nucleotide-binding catalytic core and highlights the C-terminal interaction/anchoring segment

sae_features.label: Nucleotide/phosphate-binding cleft detector
sae_features.description: Nucleotide/phosphate-binding catalytic cleft detector across ATP-hydrolyzing and phosphate-transferring enzymes, with strongest enrichment for the actin/hexokinase/Hsp70 (ASKHA) superfamily, but also robust activation for non-ASKHA phosphate-processing families that present Gly/Lys/Ser/Thr-rich phosphate-binding pockets (e.g., E-NTPDase/apyrases, DHH/DHHA1 exopolyphosphatases, PRPP synthases).

sae_features.label: Actin/Hsp70 N-terminal phosphate-binding loop
sae_features.description: N-terminal phosphate-binding loop and flanking β/α elements of the actin-like ATPase fold (Hsp70/110/Grp170, actin/ARPs), with spillover to analogous glycine/threonine/lysine-rich, phosphate-interacting N-terminal subregions in diverse EC 3.6.x enzymes and cytosolic domains that interface with ATPases

sae_features.label: N-terminal phosphate/metal-binding loops
sae_features.description: Early-domain small/polar-rich strand–loop–helix modules and first-structured segments of the mature chain that coordinate oxoanions, nucleotides, or metal ions (plus first TM helices). The feature targets the first well-structured element after any signal/disordered tail, and, in catalytic proteins, also highlights internal phosphate/metal-binding loops adjacent to active sites.

sae_features.label: Nucleotide phosphate-binding clefts
sae_features.description: Phosphate-binding glycine-rich/basic clefts of nucleotide- and phosphate-handling proteins, predominantly the actin/Hsp70/ASKHA-like ATPase/kinase cores that bind/hydrolyze NTPs or mediate phosphoryl transfer, but also other enzymes and nucleic-acid factors whose active/recognition sites engage nucleotide phosphates, polyphosphate, SAM, or nucleic-acid phosphate backbones

sae_features.label: Helical coupling lid subdomains
sae_features.description: Helical “lid/coupling” subdomains associated with nucleotide‑phosphate turnover: long alpha‑helical regions that lie downstream of the catalytic NTP/polyphosphate‑binding motifs and mediate conformational closure, oligomerization, or interaction with partner ATPases in kinases, NTPases, nucleotidyltransferases, and apyrases

sae_features.label: Gly-rich phosphate-binding loops
sae_features.description: Gly/charged contiguous loops adjacent to phosphate- or pyrophosphate-bearing nucleotide/cofactor binding elements, covering ATP/GTP sites (actin/Hsp70, tubulin/FtsZ, P-loop NTPases, protein kinases, P-type ATPases) and nucleotide-derived cofactors (SAM, NAD/FAD, CoA) in methyltransferases and acyl-transfer enzymes; occasionally extends to metal-coordinating acidic loops in vWA/MIDAS-type adhesins.
